title: 149 Mathematics Fig. 3   Fig. 4
subject: Mathematics
unit: Unit L - cardboard as shown in Fig
objective:
To divide a fraction by a natural number. [say,   1
3   ÷   4]
materials: White paper sheet, colour pens/pencils, eraser etc.
procedure:
1.   Draw a rectangle on a paper and divide it into 3 equal parts (Fig. 1).
Fig. 1
2.   Again divide each smaller rectangle (cell) into four equal parts and
obtain 12 smaller equal parts (Fig. 2).
Fig. 2
observation:
1.   Each part, in the rectangle in Fig.1, represents the fraction   1
3   .
discussion:
This activity is useful in explaining the division of two fractions.
150 Laboratory Manual – Elementary Stage
